# Types

## Overview

Frontend TypeScript type definitions. Generated types mirror the backend — never edit them manually.

## Structure

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `generated.ts` | **AUTO-GENERATED** — backend types synced to frontend |
| `index.ts` | Barrel export + custom frontend types |

## generated.ts

**NEVER edit this file manually.** It is regenerated by:

```bash
node nara generate:types
```

Contains: `User`, `Role`, `Permission`, `Session`, pagination types, and helper functions that mirror `app/core/types.ts`.

## Adding Custom Types

Add custom frontend-only types to a new file, then re-export from `index.ts`:

```typescript
// resources/js/types/forms.ts
export interface CreateProductForm {
  name: string;
  price: number;
}

// resources/js/types/index.ts
export * from "./generated";
export * from "./forms"; // add here
```

## Import Pattern

```typescript
// In pages and components:
import type { User, PaginationMeta } from "../types";
```

## Conventions

- **Never edit `generated.ts`** — always regenerate via CLI
- Custom types → new `.ts` file → export from `index.ts`
- Run `node nara generate:types` after changing backend `app/core/types.ts`
